summaries.oneLine: Encodes bidirectional allow/disallow permissions used by inclusion and solver checks.
summaries.paragraph: Implements a directional permission algebra used throughout constraint solving. The module defines the private `allowed` and `disallowed` states, pair constructors for left/right/both outcomes, and a generic `Allow_disallow` style signature for transforming permission-bearing values while preserving variance. It is used by type, mode, and jkind logic to raise or lower obligations safely.

summaries.oneLine: Public signatures for directional permission states and transform combinators.
summaries.paragraph: Declares the core permission carrier types `allowed`, `disallowed`, `left_only`, `right_only`, and `both`, together with constrained aliases for mixed products. The module exposes `Allow_disallow` with projection and projection-modifying functions, plus `Magic_allow_disallow` as a reusable functor for deriving allow/disallow operations for caller-defined side-indexed types.

summaries.oneLine: Defines annotation identifiers used when generating `.annot` metadata.
summaries.paragraph: Specifies compact annotation tags used by annotation generation: `call` describes evaluation style (`Tail`, `Stack`, `Inline`) while `ident` tracks whether an identifier occurrence is an external reference or an internal definition. The definitions support distinguishing defining sites from references so annotation emitters can build precise source maps.

summaries.oneLine: Implements core operations for mutable type representations, rows, and class-type inspection.
summaries.paragraph: Provides internal machinery for constructing and manipulating type expressions, including scoped constructors, fresh variables, abstraction helpers, traversal and copying, class signature extraction, row manipulation, and abbreviation memoization. It includes genericization routines, row/label scanning, variant hashing, polymorphic type folding, and safeguards for optional, native representation, and mode-aware type handling.

summaries.oneLine: Core API for inspecting and constructing type expressions, rows, and class signatures.
summaries.paragraph: Exposes constructors and accessors for transient and regular type objects: creating fresh vars, checking descriptors, traversing type trees, folding rows/fields, copying expressions, managing rows and row names, and instantiating generic declarations. It also exposes fixed-row checks, class signature introspection, and utilities used by unification and pattern matching logic.

summaries.oneLine: Traverses typed trees to emit annotation records for .annot files.
summaries.paragraph: Implements a Tast iterator-based pass that records identifier and node events while descending over typed expressions, patterns, modules, and structure items. It tracks binders and references, resolves identifiers through environments, and emits `Stypes` annotations describing definitions and uses with source locations suitable for .annot generation.

summaries.oneLine: Single entry point to generate annotation payloads from `.cmt` inputs.
summaries.paragraph: Declares `gen_annot` as the high-level annotation production function. It accepts source/target names and precomputed summaries, orchestrates typed-tree traversal, and outputs accumulated annotations that describe nodes, identifier definitions, and references for downstream tools that consume `.annot` output.

summaries.oneLine: Implements unification, instantiation, and type-expression transformation routines.
summaries.paragraph: Contains the central type-constraint machinery used by the checker: scoped variable creation, level and scope management, expression and declaration instantiation, expansion and canonicalization, apply/closure logic, generalization helpers, row/constructor normalization, and object/class-field operations. It also controls temporary contexts (`with_local_level`) and emits detailed mode and error context for failures.

summaries.oneLine: Public surface for unify and typing-core type operations.
summaries.paragraph: Defines exceptions for unification and subtype failures, scope guards (`with_local_level`, `with_level`), constructors for primitive and user types, generalization entry points, instance and expansion helpers, currying and apply routines, and environment-driven checks for class and constructor treatment. It is the API used by type inference phases during constraint propagation.

summaries.oneLine: Builds runtime representation views of constructor and label information.
summaries.paragraph: Implements helpers to inspect data representation metadata from type declarations. It resolves constructor tags, distinguishes boxed and unboxed layouts, enumerates extension constructors, and extracts fields and labels for printer, optimizer, and backend-facing routines that need concrete representation details during typing.

summaries.oneLine: Exposes label and constructor lookup helpers for representation-sensitive code.
summaries.paragraph: Defines APIs to obtain extension descriptors, labels for boxed or unboxed records, constructors by tag, and constructor existential content. It defines a `Constr_not_found` failure to keep representation lookups explicit and recoverable during translation or warning/reporting passes.

summaries.oneLine: Environment core for lookups, normalization, usage tracking, and required globals.
summaries.paragraph: Implements the full environment abstraction consumed by the typing pipeline. It stores summaries, resolves values/types/classes/modules, offers shadowing and shape lookups, normalizes module/value/modtype paths, tracks required globals, probes, usage marks, constructor and label access, and handles local constraint detection for incremental checks.

summaries.oneLine: Interface for main typing environment operations and query primitives.
summaries.paragraph: Declares environment data types, constructors, lookup predicates, and update hooks. The module exposes summary-level and persistent lookup paths for values, types, classes, modules, and jkinds, plus facilities for path normalization, usage marking, global requirement tracking, and error-tolerant access used by checker clients.

summaries.oneLine: Utilities for rebuilding environments and emitting environment-construction errors.
summaries.paragraph: Implements environment reconstruction helpers from summaries with configurable missing-module handling, plus cache maintenance entry points used by interactive or incremental sessions. It centralizes environment error construction and reporting, allowing higher layers to recover a partially valid typing environment instead of aborting hard on auxiliary metadata mismatches.

summaries.oneLine: API for constructing `Env.t` values from summaries and reporting failures.
summaries.paragraph: Defines helpers to create full environments from summaries, rebuild only-summary variants, and reset internal caches. Also introduces environment construction errors and printer functions so import and recovery paths can present detailed diagnostics while still continuing with partially reconstructed typing state.

summaries.oneLine: Builds and transforms structured traces for typing error explanations.
summaries.paragraph: Implements trace algebra for errors, storing ordered diff sequences, trace positions, expansions, and escape contexts. It supports mapping over traces, swapping sides, and assembling unification, equality, comparison, and subtype error records, which downstream modules format into actionable compiler messages with direction-aware details.

summaries.oneLine: Defines reusable error-trace data structures and constructors.
summaries.paragraph: Declares the generic trace model used by the type checker: positional markers, diff elements, expanded types, escape metadata, and error envelopes for unification, equality, comparison, and subtype cases. Provides mappers and swap helpers so diagnostic producers can render both failure sides consistently.

summaries.oneLine: Tracks parameterized global module names and substitution maps.
summaries.paragraph: Implements a typed representation for global module identifiers, including argument lists, hidden argument control, precision markers, and substitution maps. Supports creating, parsing, formatting, completeness checks, and deterministic substitutions so parameterized imports can be resolved and rewritten during signature reading and caching.

summaries.oneLine: Interface for parameterized global-module names and substitutions.
summaries.paragraph: Declares module/argument/name value types for globally addressed modules, constructors, and substitution maps. Exposes predicates for complete bindings, argument operations, stringification, and substitution application, forming a stable contract for persistent environments and package import code that operates on names rather than raw strings.

summaries.oneLine: Implements scoped identifier creation, hashing, and lookup tables.
summaries.paragraph: Implements identifier values with scope and stamp management, constructors for local/global/predef/instance names, renaming facilities, scope queries, and printable representations. It includes a generic identifier table abstraction with add/find/iterate operations used across environments and name-mapping passes.

summaries.oneLine: Public API for scoped identifiers and identifier tables.
summaries.paragraph: Defines the identifier ADT, predicates for global/predef/instance classification, string and scope accessors, key generation helpers, and hash-table style containers. Includes printing functions and table operations (`find_name`, iteration, folding) needed by environment maintenance and compiler diagnostics.

summaries.oneLine: Checks inclusion compatibility of class types and declarations.
summaries.paragraph: Implements class-level inclusion between class types, class signatures, and class declarations. It verifies methods, fields, visibility, and inheritance constraints, accumulates mismatches, and formats detailed class inclusion diagnostics for callers that need structured include compatibility during signature checking.

summaries.oneLine: Interfaces for class and class-type inclusion checks.
summaries.paragraph: Declares entry points for including class types, class type declarations, and class declarations, plus reporting functions for inclusion mismatches. This module is the class-specific bridge used by generic inclusion checks to validate structural compatibility and emit actionable compiler diagnostics.

summaries.oneLine: Implements core mismatch modeling and reporting for item inclusion.
summaries.paragraph: Provides foundational comparison routines for module value/type declarations, covering primitive, record, variant, constructor, privacy, mode, and jkind differences. It normalizes modal axes, compares unsafe mode crossings, generates diagnostic patches, and produces human-oriented reports consumed by module signature inclusion.

summaries.oneLine: Core inclusion mismatch definitions and reporting hooks.
summaries.paragraph: Declares mismatch descriptors for values, types, records, variants, constructors, jkinds, and mode compatibility. Also exposes comparison and reporting entry points for inclusion logic, including mode-crossing checks and utility functions to compute child-mode behavior for nested signatures.

summaries.oneLine: Implements module signature and functor inclusion validation.
summaries.paragraph: Implements full module inclusion checking, handling signatures, modtypes, strengthened declarations, functor applications, and compunit inclusion with mode-aware contexts. It builds structured explanations for failures, supports optional patches and functor application diagnostics, and emits coercions for successful inclusion paths.

summaries.oneLine: Module inclusion APIs and error model for signatures and functors.
summaries.paragraph: Defines module inclusion operations for signatures, modtypes, signature coercions, and functor equivalence checks. Also declares explanation/error types and helpers that allow printers to render actionable include failures, plus utilities for checking implementations under mode-tracking contexts.

summaries.oneLine: Renders detailed inclusion diagnostics into the compiler printer registry.
summaries.paragraph: Implements printer registration and formatting of `Includemod` explanation trees. The module centralizes user-facing text for signature and functor inclusion mismatches, making error rendering consistent regardless of caller and ensuring complex mismatch trees are displayed in a stable structure.

summaries.oneLine: Exports printers for inclusion explanation trees and registration hook.
summaries.paragraph: Declares the pretty-printer for inclusion explanation values and a registration function. Its purpose is to wire human-readable reporting for inclusion errors into global printing infrastructure before they are surfaced to users.

summaries.oneLine: Implements Jkind construction, intersection, conversion, and diagnostics.
summaries.paragraph: Implements the complete Jkind model, including sort handling, layout and bound management, product decomposition, normalization, subtyping/intersection logic, and axis constraint manipulation. It also tracks externality/nullability/separability bounds, prints verbose histories, converts annotations, and raises user errors through structured violation channels.

summaries.oneLine: Public Jkind API for construction, normalization, and compatibility checks.
summaries.paragraph: Defines the Jkind interface around modeed sort axes, bounds, const constructors, history, and base/axis decomposition. It exposes builders for common jkinds, bounds extraction, normalization entrypoints, formatting hooks, intersection/subtyping operations, and error-safe comparison utilities used during typing and codegen checks.

summaries.oneLine: Implements per-axis algebra for externality, nullability, and separability.
summaries.paragraph: Provides generic axis machinery for jkind components, including axis operations, axis-set composition, and per-axis conversions. It packages externality, nullability, and separability modules with wrappers that are later assembled by the main Jkind logic into full product semantics.

summaries.oneLine: Axis interfaces for per-axis Jkind constraints and ordered axis sets.
summaries.paragraph: Defines a generic axis signature, axis-set abstraction, and concrete axis modules for externality, nullability, and separability. These interfaces support ordered comparison, packing/unpacking axes, and building per-axis operations used by Jkind products and diagnostics.

summaries.oneLine: Shared Jkind interface contract to avoid duplicated sort definitions.
summaries.paragraph: Introduces base module types for Jkind sorts, unification variables, and constant sorts. It specifies product and variable representations, formatting functions, and debug hooks, providing a small, stable contract that downstream modules can depend on without importing concrete implementation details.

summaries.oneLine: Defines concrete Jkind sort and layout type definitions used by the compiler.
summaries.paragraph: Implements concrete types and aliases for sort, layout, and axis descriptors, linking Jkind sort constants to shape and representation categories. It provides the concrete building blocks that both parser translation and type/shape printers rely on to represent value-layout intent.

summaries.oneLine: Canonical type definitions for Jkind sort/layout structures.
summaries.paragraph: Declares the public Jkind type families for sort and layout information, including base constructors and helper projections. Consumers use these types when building, comparing, and printing jkind-related classification in typing and error reporting code.

summaries.oneLine: Implements mode lattices, hints, and solver-facing adjunction machinery.
summaries.paragraph: Implements the actual mode lattice model used by inference: morphism adjoints, constant constructors, allow/disallow transforms, level handling, submode and equate operations, and zapping/generalization behavior. It wires `Mode_hint` explanations into solver proofs so diagnostics carry precise context for mode failures.

summaries.oneLine: Defines mode interfaces, constructors, and submode/equate APIs.
summaries.paragraph: Exports the public mode module API: lattice behavior, constant and variable operations, polymorphic axis handling, hint-aware submode checks, equate and generalization functions, and level-sensitive conversions. It also includes user-facing printers and error conversion utilities for mode checking diagnostics.

summaries.oneLine: Stores mode diagnostics hints, morphisms, and pinpoint provenance.
summaries.paragraph: Defines hint structures used to explain why mode constraints fail, including pinpoint locations, morphisms, polarity constructors, and direction-sensitive constants. These hints feed into mode comparison routines to generate detailed messages while preserving location context and composition of nested mode transitions.

summaries.oneLine: Interface contracts for all mode lattices and constraint solvers.
summaries.paragraph: Provides generic interfaces for lattice structure, constants, axis comparisons, and bidirectional mode types with left/right/dual flavors. It specifies submode and equate semantics, hinted errors, zapping operations, and printing behavior; this contract is reused across allocation, locality, value, and other mode modules.

summaries.oneLine: Implements module-type normalization, strengthening, and nondependent filtering.
summaries.paragraph: Implements rewriting and reduction of module types including alias scrapes, strengthens, enrich operations, nondependent signatures, and manifest expansion. Also computes module type paths and handles codegen visibility checks, helping the module typer collapse unnecessary wrappers and preserve sharing correctness.

summaries.oneLine: API for module-type manipulation and simplification functions.
summaries.paragraph: Declares operations for scraping/reducing types, strengthening aliases, nondependent signature filtering, enrichment, path computation, and code-need checks. The interface centralizes module-type hygiene routines invoked during functor typing and interface consistency checks.

summaries.oneLine: Mutable printers for type-oriented printing callbacks.
summaries.paragraph: Implements mutable references to printer functions for identifiers, values, labels, modes, jkinds, and general type/module trees. It gives the typing subsystem configurable pretty-print hooks so backends and tooling can replace printers dynamically without recompiling core logic.

summaries.oneLine: Printer hook references for values, types, and module signatures.
summaries.paragraph: Declares mutable printer references and helper predicates that describe how identifiers should be parenthesized. It exports generic printer aliases for outcometree nodes and exposes configurable sinks used by type and pattern printing components.

summaries.oneLine: Intermediate tree representation for all printer-facing typed outputs.
summaries.paragraph: Defines the `outcometree` structural model for types, identifiers, constants, modes, classes, modules, and phrases before textual rendering. The types are intentionally printer-oriented and decouple semantic typing data from direct `Format` formatting details.

summaries.oneLine: Implements pattern coverage and least-upper-bound calculations for match checking.
summaries.paragraph: Implements partial order checks and pattern utilities used for match exhaustiveness: constants compare, pattern compatibility, least upper bound computation, variant pressure tracking, partial and irrefutable checks, and compatibility between typed/untyped cases. It also tracks special constant handling for float32 and active/inactive cases.

summaries.oneLine: Pattern matching comparison and coverage-check interfaces.
summaries.paragraph: Defines typed/untyped pattern types used by the matcher, constructors for compatibility checks, utilities to compute `lub`, pressure variants, and functions to check partiality and irrefutability. It also exposes helpers for constructing and transforming case lists during pattern analysis.

summaries.oneLine: Implements path normalization, comparison, and formatting helpers.
summaries.paragraph: Implements the OCaml path abstraction: equality and ordering, conversion to readable names with parenthesization control, free-variable detection, flattening and scope queries, constructor-path recognition, and generated `Map`/`Set` modules. These operations are shared by environment resolution and pretty-printing.

summaries.oneLine: Public API for canonical identifier path operations.
summaries.paragraph: Declares path constructors and utility functions for scope, comparison, flattening, printing, and lookup on identifier chains. Also exposes `Map` and `Set` instantiations and helpers for distinguishing constructor-style paths from general module/type paths.

summaries.oneLine: Implements omega and row-pattern templates used by matching analyses.
summaries.paragraph: Provides reusable wildcard-pattern values and constructors (`omega`, `omegas`) plus specialized modules for non-empty rows, simple, half-simple, general, and head-only pattern families. It helps the matcher and exhaustiveness passes build canonical placeholder patterns and compare row shapes.

summaries.oneLine: Pattern-template API for wildcard rows and structural row helpers.
summaries.paragraph: Exposes wildcard constructors for pattern synthesis and pattern families for non-empty row structures. Submodules cover simple, half-simple, general, and head-only row strategies that support matching and exhaustiveness logic in typed pattern analysis.

summaries.oneLine: Implements persistent signature caching, loading, and import bookkeeping.
summaries.paragraph: Implements the persistent environment abstraction that stores and retrieves typed signatures, manages cmi generation/loading, tracks imported/opaque parameters, normalizes names, records CRCs, and executes delayed checks. It also exposes hooks for hidden/opaque parameter handling and cache lifecycle control.

summaries.oneLine: Persistent environment interface for CMI-backed signatures and imports.
summaries.paragraph: Declares the persistent signature table, parameter/import utilities, lookup strategies, and can-load policy controls. Includes operations for reading, saving, checking, and normalizing globals, and for tracking quoted imports, runtime parameters, delayed loading checks, and import metadata.

summaries.oneLine: Builds and registers all predefined type expressions and built-in identifiers.
summaries.paragraph: Implements construction of the compiler’s canonical predefined types, constructors, SIMD types, and path constants. It also registers these entries in the initial environment, populates builtin identifier maps, and defines convenience constructors for frequently used `type_expr` wrappers.

summaries.oneLine: Predefined types, paths, identifiers, and registration helpers exposed by typing.
summaries.paragraph: Declares the universe of predefined builtins: scalar types, SIMD vectors, option/list/array constructors, exceptional identifiers, and helper functions to add extension types. It also publishes built-in identifier/path tables and `or_null` helpers required by runtime typing rules.

summaries.oneLine: Implements primitive metadata for representation, effects, and coeffects.
summaries.paragraph: Implements primitive descriptor parsing and validation. It models numeric/vector layouts, boxed/unboxed variants, effect classes, and printing helpers for native/bytecode names. It also supports equality/comparison, validity checks, and robust error reporting when primitive annotations are inconsistent.

summaries.oneLine: Defines primitive descriptors, layouts, effects, and validation API.
summaries.paragraph: Declares abstract representation descriptors, boxed/unboxed variants, effect/coeffect enums, and conversion helpers. Provides parser and printer functions plus predicates for comparing and validating primitives, including checks that ensure representation choices are coherent during lowering and typing.

summaries.oneLine: Pretty-prints typed patterns and constants for diagnostics.
summaries.paragraph: Implements formatting routines for constants and patterns used in typed output and compatibility mode. It converts internal pattern representations into printable forms, supports generic and top-level printers, and provides compatibility shims so downstream tooling can use the same rendering style.

summaries.oneLine: Pattern pretty-printing entry points and compatibility helpers.
summaries.paragraph: Declares pattern pretty-printers including constant and top-level pattern output functions. It also exposes compatibility wrappers to keep pattern rendering stable across printer backends and diagnostic styles.

summaries.oneLine: Implements comprehensive type and signature printing plus error renderers.
summaries.paragraph: Defines converters from internal types to outcometree, type and value declaration printers, and helpers for prepared/shared printing to avoid exponential output. It coordinates path naming, jkind/mode rendering, modality-aware signatures, and specific reporters for unification, equality, and subtype errors.

summaries.oneLine: Type-printer API for outcometree conversion and diagnostic rendering.
summaries.paragraph: Declares path/type/class/module printers, converters from typed declarations to printable trees, preparation helpers for shared representations, and reporting functions for core type errors. Also includes ambiguity checks and compatibility printer modules used across compiler user messages.

summaries.oneLine: Renders typed interfaces and implementations through typedtree printers.
summaries.paragraph: Implements typed output generation for signatures and structures, with support for applying explicit coercions when needed. It centralizes the final pretty-print path used by diagnostic tooling and debugging output for fully typed sources.

summaries.oneLine: Public typed-tree printing functions for signatures and implementations.
summaries.paragraph: Exports entrypoints to print a typed signature or implementation and variants that accept coercion context. This enables command-line and tooling workflows to render typedtree artifacts in a stable and compiler-consistent textual form.

summaries.oneLine: Implements numeric scalar traits for integer and floating representations.
summaries.paragraph: Defines scalar metadata classes with width, locality mode, signedness, and comparison families. It provides constructors and accessors for integral and floating representations, used by layout selection, optimization heuristics, and backend code generation decisions.

summaries.oneLine: Scalar type traits, operations, and comparison interfaces.
summaries.paragraph: Declares scalar abstractions, including width and signedness categories, integer/float variants, and operation traits for comparisons and conversions. These interfaces support uniform handling of numeric forms throughout type optioning and code generation.

summaries.oneLine: Defines symbolic shape language for signatures and hashing equivalence.
summaries.paragraph: Implements shape construction and reduction primitives for type and signature identity. It supports uid allocation, path projection, row and record constructors, recursion (`mu`/rec_var), and mapping/decomposition helpers. Shapes are printable, comparable, and used for incremental compilation and cacheable dependency checks.

summaries.oneLine: Shape construction, decomposition, and equality interfaces for type signatures.
summaries.paragraph: Declares shape descriptors, path and uid management, item maps, component kinds, and constructors for tuples, variants, objects, aliases, and projections. It also exposes equality, printing, normalization, and table hooks used by signature shape registries.

summaries.oneLine: Performs bounded, fuel-aware shape reduction with diagnostics.
summaries.paragraph: Implements a call-by-need evaluator that reduces shapes under configurable limits. It tracks reduction steps, cms file lookups, cache hits/misses, unresolved aliases, and approximations; supports diagnostics collection, and provides local reducers that can be configured by compiler mode or Merlin settings.

summaries.oneLine: Shape reduction interfaces, diagnostics, and configurable reducer constructors.
summaries.paragraph: Declares reduction result forms, optional diagnostics, and a functor-based reducer parameterized by fuel and CMS-read callbacks. Exposes `local_reduce` and UID-specific reduction entry points used before signature comparison and export checks.

summaries.oneLine: Organizes signatures into recursive and non-recursive groups for pass scheduling.
summaries.paragraph: Implements grouping utilities over signature items, distinguishing recursive cores and producing sequential group iterators. It supports traversal (`seq`, `iter`, `fold`), conversion back to flat signatures, and patch-like in-place replacement for recursive-group aware transformations.

summaries.oneLine: Recursive-group typing for signature item streams.
summaries.paragraph: Declares grouped signature item representations, iterators over recursive groups, and replacement utilities for in-place patching. It exposes both sequence and fold styles to support passes that need group boundaries during validation or rewriting.

summaries.oneLine: Carries signature data together with global binding metadata.
summaries.paragraph: Implements a compact wrapper for signatures with captured global binding context. It supports loading from CMI lazy infos and substitution over global parameter bindings so modules with parameterized imports retain enough metadata for accurate resolution.

summaries.oneLine: Interface for signatures annotated with global binding context.
summaries.paragraph: Declares a private record type containing a signature and associated global bindings. Provides read and substitution operations to safely rewrite global references while preserving auxiliary binding metadata for linker or serialization paths.

summaries.oneLine: Implements generic hint-guided solver infrastructure.
summaries.paragraph: Implements the solver core for monotone equations over lattices and hinted morphisms. It supports branch hints, composed transformations, left/right adjoints, allow/disallow toggling, and population/normalization of hints to derive proofs for constraint solving in mode and jkind analyses.

summaries.oneLine: Generalized solver signatures for hints, lattices, and monomorphic solvers.
summaries.paragraph: Declares lattice and hint typeclasses, comparison/equality abstractions, branch/error structures, and the monomorphic solver module type used across modalities. Exposes a common contract so constraint systems can be instantiated with different constants and morphisms.

summaries.oneLine: Solver framework contracts and helper interfaces for hint-driven solving.
summaries.paragraph: Defines the formal interfaces for solver objects, including lattice laws, morphism behavior, comparison/equality witnesses, and solver traits. It is the abstract contract that allows typing and mode systems to share constraint machinery without depending on concrete implementations.

summaries.oneLine: Records and dumps typed-annotation event streams.
summaries.paragraph: Implements runtime recording of typed-stree annotation entries for identifier occurrences and node types. It stores location-bearing events and provides dumping and retrieval utilities used by annot generation and downstream tooling that consumes type source metadata.

summaries.oneLine: Annotation event record API for `.cmt`/`.annot` workflows.
summaries.paragraph: Declares the structured annotation type and functions to record and retrieve annotations by location, identifier, and payload kind. Supports dumping collected annotations and extracting annotation locations for consumers that map typedtree events to external metadata.

summaries.oneLine: Implements safe and unsafe substitutions over typed declarations and signatures.
summaries.paragraph: Implements identifier/path substitutions for values, types, modules, modtypes, and jkinds across expressions, declarations, and signatures. It tracks safe/unsafe substitution modes, optional location rewrites, composition, scoped rewriting, and lazy substitutions for large imports.

summaries.oneLine: Substitution API for types, paths, and declaration structures.
summaries.paragraph: Defines typed substitution types plus operations to rewrite identifiers and full declarations across type expressions, classes, modules, signatures, and modtypes. Exposes unsafe conversion, additional action hooks, location remapping, and composition utilities.

summaries.oneLine: Traverser over typed AST nodes with a customizable no-op default.
summaries.paragraph: Implements the default typed-tree iterator with callback records for expressions, patterns, classes, and structures. It enables compiler passes to plug into specific nodes while retaining standard recursive traversal behavior across the full typed syntax tree.

summaries.oneLine: Iterator record and default traversal for typed AST data structures.
summaries.paragraph: Declares the typed iterator record and default instance. Exposes traversal entry points for recursive descent over typed nodes so checks or analyses can selectively override visit behavior without rewriting full navigation logic.

summaries.oneLine: Typedtree transformation utilities with map-style callbacks.
summaries.paragraph: Implements a default mapper that recursively transforms typed AST nodes, enabling targeted rewrites over expressions, patterns, classes, structures, and signatures. The module is used for local tree rewriting and adaptation when passes need to alter typing products.

summaries.oneLine: Typedmapper API for structural rewrites of typedtrees.
summaries.paragraph: Declares the mapper record and default implementation with a full suite of typedtree map callbacks. It provides a high-level interface for systematic AST transformations without manual recursion boilerplate.

summaries.oneLine: Computes and caches symbolic shapes for type declarations.
summaries.paragraph: Implements type-shape extraction into UID-keyed tables, tracks evaluated shapes with layout metadata, and exposes debug printers for all known tables. This supports consistency checks, incremental signatures, and tools needing a compact symbolic view of type structures.

summaries.oneLine: Caching and diagnostics interface for declaration shape computation.
summaries.paragraph: Declares UID-indexed registries for all type declarations and computed value shapes. Provides APIs to add, fetch, print, and debug shape tables, plus optional diagnostics hooks for shape lookup and evaluation.

summaries.oneLine: Checks and builds class declarations, signatures, and coercions.
summaries.paragraph: Implements class declaration typing, inheritance integration, method and instance-variable insertion, delayed method type checks, class signature completion, and recmod/class consistency checks. It also reports detailed class errors and manages approximations used during recursive class binding.

summaries.oneLine: Class declaration and class-type translation APIs.
summaries.paragraph: Declares class typing entrypoints for class declarations, descriptions, type declarations, approximations, and recursive class checks. It also exposes class error types and reporting functions used when class types are built or included.

summaries.oneLine: Implements the main expression/type core checker entrypoints.
summaries.paragraph: Contains the principal type-checking logic for expressions, let bindings, patterns, modules, classes, and packages. It performs recursive-check orchestration, delayed-check flushing, allocation tracking, pattern naming, mode forcing, and raises structured errors for unsupported or invalid recursive constructions.

summaries.oneLine: Core typing API for expressions, modules, classes, and error reporting.
summaries.paragraph: Declares type-checking entry functions for lets, expressions, patterns, classes, options, and modules, as well as delayed-check management and environment-facing helpers. Also defines recursive-support flags, unsupported allocation cases, and the central error/reporting interface.

summaries.oneLine: Translates and validates type declarations and extensions.
summaries.paragraph: Implements translation of type declarations, exceptions, type extensions, and value declarations from syntax to internal types. Performs coherence and fixed-type checks, handles mixed-product and native representation constraints, resolves jkind annotations, and packages related errors for reporting.

summaries.oneLine: Type declaration translation API for parsed declarations and extensions.
summaries.paragraph: Provides functions to translate type declarations, exceptions, value declarations, constraints, and jkind declarations from syntax trees. Includes helper checks for fixed types, mixed-product support, native repr kinds, and cohesive error reporting for malformed declarations.

summaries.oneLine: Generic framework to compute and attach declaration properties.
summaries.paragraph: Implements property descriptors for declarations with optional input requirements and support for computing cached property values in bulk. It standardizes recomputation flow and ensures declaration metadata is updated consistently across typing passes.

summaries.oneLine: Declares property abstraction and computation helpers for type declarations.
summaries.paragraph: Defines a property object with associated request metadata, generic compute functions, and utilities to derive declaration annotations. Serves as a shared mechanism for attaching variance, separability, and other computed facts to type declarations.

summaries.oneLine: Computes separability classifications for declarations.
summaries.paragraph: Implements separability inference and validation for recursive declaration groups. It analyzes declaration signatures, computes separability modes, updates declaration metadata, and raises errors when a declaration violates structural or modal separability assumptions.

summaries.oneLine: Separability property API and update flow for type declarations.
summaries.paragraph: Declares separability modes, errors, and the `compute_decl` API that derives declaration-level separability. Exposes the registered property and an `update_decls` flow for integrating results into typing environments.

summaries.oneLine: Infers and validates variance for type parameters and class fields.
summaries.paragraph: Implements variance derivation from declarations and class declarations, tracks variance variables, checks extension constraints, and updates declaration metadata after analysis. It also exposes targeted error handling for mismatched or unsupported variance scenarios.

summaries.oneLine: Variance request and computation interfaces for declarations.
summaries.paragraph: Declares variance request/result representations and public APIs for computing variance from type declarations or class declarations. Includes error constructors, update routines, and helper to inspect variance variables against required properties.

summaries.oneLine: Defines the compiler's typed AST node representations and helper utilities.
summaries.paragraph: Implements the typed tree used by the typechecker and downstream passes: expressions, patterns, class and module constructs, modes/modalities, typed patterns, allocation markers, and utility traversal functions. It also defines helpers for unique-use modeling, arity extraction, and pattern predicate filters.

summaries.oneLine: Public schema for typed expressions, patterns, and related utilities.
summaries.paragraph: Declares the full typed-tree types including pattern categories, modes, signatures, structures, and expression/class declarations. Provides helper functions for traversal, mapping, classification, location extraction, binding-id collection, and formatting of alloc and uniqueness metadata.

summaries.oneLine: Implements module typing of structures, interfaces, and top-level phrases.
summaries.paragraph: Implements top-level module typing orchestration for structures, interfaces, implementations, opens, and package units. It coordinates signature persistence, environment initialization, non-generalized signature checking, and emits typed module expressions for later backend phases.

summaries.oneLine: High-level module typing entry points and error handling types.
summaries.paragraph: Declares entrypoints for typing modules and interfaces, package/unit opening, initial environment construction, and top-level phrase handling. Also defines hiding and dependency error types plus reporting functions used by callers to surface module-level typing failures.

summaries.oneLine: Translates modal annotations from syntax into internal mode values.
summaries.paragraph: Implements annotation translation for alloc and modality axes, including defaulting rules, deduplication, immutable/mutable axis handling, and untranslation for round-tripping. It also exposes builders for index modalities and jkind bounds used by downstream checkers.

summaries.oneLine: Public APIs for translating and untranslating mode annotations.
summaries.paragraph: Defines functions to translate parser mode nodes into internal mode constants and modalities, compute defaults, build mutable-axis values, and convert internal constructs back to attributes. Supports both explicit and fallback coercion paths used by annotation checking and output printing.

summaries.oneLine: Classifies arrays, layouts, and backend-oriented value kinds from types.
summaries.paragraph: Implements type-based optimization hooks to choose pointer-like tests, array element kinds, mutability categories, specialized layouts for float/simd types, and function/lazy argument layouts. It also classifies scalar/lazy cases and returns lambda-friendly value kinds.

summaries.oneLine: Type-option APIs for layout, array kind, and value-kind selection.
summaries.paragraph: Declares predicates and constructors used during optimization lowering: function and base-type checks, array kind inference, pointer classification, large-block layout selection, lazy argument analysis, and value kind determination based on mutability and sort.

summaries.oneLine: Core mutable type representation, declaration, and variance data model.
summaries.paragraph: Implements the concrete runtime type structures used by the checker: atoms, rows, fields, class shapes, mutability/variance/separability enums, and helper operations over all representation components. This file is the definitive implementation of `Types` internals.

summaries.oneLine: Public definitions for the mutable type representation and metadata.
summaries.paragraph: Declares mutability, atomicity, class type descriptors, row and field views, abstraction wrappers, type identifiers, variance types, and a comprehensive public surface for constructing and querying typed declarations, rows, constructors, and class metadata.

summaries.oneLine: Translates type syntax to internal type expressions and handles modtype constraints.
summaries.paragraph: Implements parsing and conversion from `Parsetree` type forms to internal types, label translation, type schemes, type parameter and jkind extraction, and error-raised validation for invalid quantification. Also forwards module-type translators and package constraint checking helpers.

summaries.oneLine: Type-expression translation API and error reporting for parser/type interactions.
summaries.paragraph: Defines APIs for translating simple types, type schemes, modtypes, and type-variable names into internal forms. Declares error types and report functions used by parser-facing phases when type translation or mode inference fails.

summaries.oneLine: Enforces uniqueness/borrowing usage rules across expressions and bindings.
summaries.paragraph: Implements a full uniqueness analysis with multiple checker modules covering unique, aliased, usage, tags, projections, and value trees. Tracks borrow regions and overwrite operations, validates recursive usage safety, and reports errors when unique values are misused.

summaries.oneLine: Top-level uniqueness analysis entry points and policy signatures.
summaries.paragraph: Exposes APIs to run uniqueness checks on expressions and value bindings, and publishes module signatures for checkers controlling aliasing, usage, tags, projections, and paths. Also defines the environment interface used by downstream passes.

summaries.oneLine: Converts typed trees back into untyped Parsetree forms.
summaries.paragraph: Implements `Untype` traversal with customizable mapping functions, enabling structured conversion from typed expressions, patterns, structures, and signatures to untyped AST with location-preserving output. Useful for tools needing round-trip or debugging outputs in source-level syntax.

summaries.oneLine: Untype mapper types and conversion functions from typed to untyped trees.
summaries.paragraph: Declares a typed-to-untyped mapper record and default implementation. Provides functions for converting untyped views of structures, signatures, expressions, patterns, and constants while preserving source annotations.

summaries.oneLine: Validates recursive value and class binding shapes for correctness.
summaries.paragraph: Implements rules checking valid recursive value expressions and class recursive forms by validating syntactic and scoping constraints. Ensures recursive bindings that are unsafe or non-terminating by definition are rejected before codegen-level assumptions are applied.

summaries.oneLine: Predicate API to validate recursive bindings and recursive class expressions.
summaries.paragraph: Exposes checks for recursive expressions and class expressions by identifier list, letting callers determine if a value-recursive structure respects compiler validity requirements before proceeding with typing or optimization.

summaries.oneLine: Declares recursive binding kind categories for recursion checks.
summaries.paragraph: Defines the data model used by the recursion checker to categorize recursive bindings, enabling precise policy selection in compiler passes that validate fixpoint-style definitions and their legality.

summaries.oneLine: Extracts compiler-relevant typed-tree payloads for Vicuna integration.
summaries.paragraph: Implements a typed-tree traversal designed for Vicuna use-cases, producing simplified extracted data from typed constructs. It centralizes filtering and packaging of tree information for external consumption by tooling pipelines.

summaries.oneLine: Typed-tree extraction API for Vicuna tooling.
summaries.paragraph: Declares a single extraction function that consumes typed trees and emits Vicuna-compatible data structures, enabling external tools to consume compiler-typed shape and binding metadata.

summaries.oneLine: Builds and prints Vicuna-specific function-shape descriptors for externals.
summaries.paragraph: Implements value-shape modeling for extensional function values, tracks extension-function descriptors, supports readable and compact printing, and packages shape information consumed by Vicuna-oriented consumers or analytics tools.

summaries.oneLine: Value-shape and extfun data definitions for Vicuna data flow.
summaries.paragraph: Declares types for shapes, function-shape maps, and external function descriptors. Exposes constructors and printers for machine-readable and human-readable output of Vicuna-shaped metadata.

summaries.oneLine: Tracks zero-allocation assumptions and checks with rollback support.
summaries.paragraph: Implements mutable state for zero-allocation checking with creation from attributes/constants, diff/subtraction between states, and a change log with undo hooks. It prints errors and debug views and lets clients temporarily assume or reset zero-alloc conditions.

summaries.oneLine: Zero-alloc attribute/state model and difference/check APIs.
summaries.paragraph: Declares check/assume/const variants, state constructors, set-undo hooks, difference computation, and error rendering. The interface enables passes to compare zero-allocation assumptions and fail fast when attributes conflict with inferred behavior.
